# xelon_object_storage_access_key (Resource)

The object storage access key resource allows you to manage Xelon Object Storage Access Key for a user.

An access key represents an S3-compatible credential pair consisting of an access key ID and a secret key.
These credentials are used to authenticate applications and tools against Xelon Object Storage.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "xelon_object_storage_access_key" "test" {
  user_id = xelon_object_storage_user.test.id
}

resource "xelon_object_storage_user" "test" {
  name          = "testuser"
  region        = "zh1"
  storage_limit = 500
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `user_id` (String) ID of the object storage user owning this access key.

### Read-Only

- `access_key_id` (String) S3 access key ID used to authenticate requests.
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the access key was created (RFC3339).
- `id` (String) ID of the object storage access key.
- `secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) S3 secret access key used to sign requests. Only returned at creation time and cannot be retrieved afterwards.

## Import

Using `terraform import`, import the Object Storage Access Key using the format: `<user_id>/<id>`. For example:

```shell
terraform import xelon_object_storage_access_key.test 153096db-01c4-4c89-93c1-8cee2229a8f1/0d0d1416c88b
```

Note: `secret_access_key` is only returned at creation time and is not available after import.
